title_en: Amazon UK Just Opened Invite-Only Lottery Requests for Elusive Pokémon TCG ETBs and Booster Bundles, Giving Fans a Rare Chance to Secure Them at RRP
summary_en: Amazon UK has opened invite-only lottery listings for highly sought-after Pokémon TCG Elite Trainer Boxes and Booster Bundles, allowing fans a rare chance to beat scalpers and buy at retail price.
body_en:
The first invite-only deal I’ve spotted is for the Mega Evolution—Chaos Rising Elite Trainer Box, going for £44.99. This is a really rare opportunity to pick up this expansion for MSRP.
Inside, you’ll get nine booster packs from Pokémon TCG: Mega Evolution—Chaos Rising and one full-art foil promo card featuring Fennekin. Each booster pack contains 10 game cards, one Basic Energy, and one Pokémon TCG Live code card, which can switch up depending on the pack. You also get your standard ETB fare - 40 Pokémon TCG Energy cards, six damage-counter dice, 65 card sleeves, and a competition-legal coin-flip die. That’s a lot of stuff to keep organised, but fortunately you’ll also get a collector’s box in this ETB to hold everything, with 6 dividers to keep it all organized. Neat - quite literally!
Released back in May, Chaos Rising has quickly become a fan-favourite expansion because it heavily spotlights the immensely popular Kalos starter evolution lines . The undeniable crown jewel of the set list is the Special Illustration Rare (SIR) Mega Greninja ex. While opinions are divided on the grand golden artwork, it’s no surprise people still want it when Greninja, one of the most popular Pokémon of all time, is the star of the show. A lot of people have been frustrated that the packs are so hard to find at retail, which makes snagging an entire ETB at standard retail an absolutely massive score.
Next up, Mega Heracross ex, Mega Lopunny ex, and a scorching new face—Charcadet—headline Phantasmal Flames. Amazon UK has invites open for the Phantasmal Flames Elite Trainer Box. With nine booster packs, a stunning full-art Charcadet promo, and a player’s guide, the Elite Trainer Box is a Magmar-hot commodity at £44.99, down from Amazon’s listed price of £145.99.
You also get 40 Pokémon TCG Energy cards, 6 damage-counter dice, 65 card sleeves, and 1 competition-legal coin-flip die. All the ETB goodies we’ve come to expect by now!
Originally hitting store shelves in late 2025, Phantasmal Flames has held a special place in the hearts of competitive players and collectors alike. Fans love this set, as do resellers. It has some major nostalgia-bait Kanto classic chase cards; most notably the breathtaking Special Illustration Rares of Mega Charizard X ex and Mega Gengar ex.
Since factory printing allocations for this era have largely dried up, Amazon’s invite loop is one of the safest ways to get sealed packs without paying inflated secondary-market prices.
And last, but by no means least, Amazon UK has a huge score up for invite - the Pokémon TCG: Scarlet & Violet—Prismatic Evolutions Booster Bundle for £21.29.
This special mid-block expansion is entirely focused on my beloved Eevee and its iconic Eeveelutions, in a gorgeous Stellar Tera motif first introduced in Scarlet & Violet - Stellar Crown. This much-loved expansion has over 175 cards to collect, so it’s no wonder these usually fly off the shelves.
This Booster Bundle has six booster packs from Pokémon TCG: Scarlet & Violet—Prismatic Evolutions. It’s a great way to get multiple booster packs without buying them individually or getting a full booster box. Perfect if you want to hunt down any of the stunning artwork this expansion has for all of the Eeveelutions, as well as 32 Illustration Rares and five hyper rare gold-etched Pokémon ex cards.
Ever since its release, fan sentiment has been off the charts because it features an absolute treasure trove of highly collectable Secret Illustration Rares for Umbreon, Sylveon, and the rest of the crew. Because it is a special "holiday" style set, you can’t buy traditional 36-pack booster boxes. The packs are sold only as part of bundled products such as Elite Trainer Boxes, Super-Premium Collections, and Mini Tins. That means configurations like Amazon’s 6-pack Booster Bundle are the absolute most cost-effective way to hunt for the elusive Special Illustration Rare Eevee ex or the gold hyper-rares without paying for extra plastic fluff or giant premium collection boxes.
These three deals could prove super effective on my bank account - good luck in the queues! Right now, there’s no word on when you’ll know if you’re successful - in previous Amazon "Request an Invite" lottery systems I’ve entered, invites are usually sent out in waves a few times a week. But, of course, it’s a lottery, so if you don’t hear back, you’re essentially left in the dark. You can keep an eye on the r/PokemonDealsUK subreddit and see if any fellow Trainers have snagged these goods.
Sara Heritage is a freelance journalist for IGN, specialising in Pokémon TCG. When she's not gaming, you’ll probably find her building yet another LEGO set she absolutely did not need, or chaining ranked matches in Pokémon Champions.
